perturbation.original_text: (d) During the Restricted Period, Kennedy shall provide written notice as soon as reasonably possible (it being agreed that ninety (90) days prior written notice is acceptable) before (i) Kennedy or any of his Affiliates begin to engage in the Company Business excluding the medical device business or (ii) Kennedy or any of his Affiliates enter into a transaction to acquire the stock or substantially all of the assets of any Person that engages in the Company Business excluding the medical device business.
perturbation.changed_text: (d) During the Restricted Period, Kennedy shall provide written notice as soon as reasonably possible (it being agreed that thirty (30) days prior written notice is acceptable) before (i) Kennedy or any of his Affiliates begin to engage in the Company Business excluding the medical device business or (ii) Kennedy or any of his Affiliates enter into a transaction to acquire the stock or substantially all of the assets of any Person that engages in the Company Business excluding the medical device business.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes the acceptable prior written notice period from 90 days to 30 days. This introduces an in-text contradiction as it creates uncertainty regarding how much notice is considered reasonably possible and acceptable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) During the Restricted Period, Kennedy shall provide written notice as soon as reasonably possible (it being agreed that thirty (30) days prior written notice is acceptable) before (i) Kennedy or any of his Affiliates begin to engage in the Company Business excluding the medical device business or (ii) Kennedy or any of his Affiliates enter into a transaction to acquire the stock or substantially all of the assets of any Person that engages in the Company Business excluding the medical device business.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that 90 days' notice is acceptable, while the changed text reduces this to 30 days. The contradicted text retains the 30-day notice period as acceptable. Since the Changed Text modifies what is acceptable to 30 days, and the Contradicted Text also specifies that 30 days is acceptable, the new version contradicts the old version, where 90 days was acceptable. Both sections address the same condition of providing notice during the Restricted Period, but now contain conflicting information, creating an inconsistency, as defined in Perturbation Definitions #5, which leads to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (g) This Agreement embodies the entire agreement among the parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings relating to such subject matter.
perturbation.changed_text: (g) This Agreement embodies the entire agreement among the parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings relating to such subject matter, except for any prior agreements related to Kennedy's role as a stockholder of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The modification adds an exception for prior agreements related to Kennedy's role as a stockholder. This introduces an in-text contradiction because the agreement is no longer the *entire* agreement, causing uncertainty about which terms and agreements are valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (g) This Agreement embodies the entire agreement among the parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings relating to such subject matter, except for any prior agreements related to Kennedy's role as a stockholder of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement embodies the *entire* agreement. The changed text adds an exception: prior agreements related to Kennedy's role as a stockholder. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text which implies that the agreement is both the *entire* agreement and not the *entire* agreement simultaneously, creating a direct contradiction, as the document now suggests there are potentially other agreements outside of this one, creating uncertainty about which agreements are valid. This adheres to the Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction definition of conflicting obligations.
